posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Gracefully this is an infrequent occurrence, but it does seem like when it happens, it’s in lots!

Friday, a 1985 Dunn Napa Cab. Seems like poor storage got to this one somewhere along the line.

Saturday, a 2009 Huet Franc de Pied was slightly oxidized. Another was quite lovely.

Sunday, a 1995 Kalin Chard was horribly oxidized, looked like a mature Sauternes.

Today, a 2003 Joguet Chinon Clos de le Dioterie, my second one in a few weeks that is horribly stewed, likes spoiled tomatoes. I gagged.

I almost went for the Lagavulin like I did last night, but instead grabbed a Levet, which is like the Lagavulin of Côte Rotie. Sitting by the lake on a lovely breezy evening, dog at my feet, and all is forgotten, after this cathartic!
Obviously you are paying the karmic price for opening decent wines to enjoy while your bride is suffering. What did you make her for dinner tonight?

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: Brotha please. Her tennis girlfriends have made seven+ nights of meals. Every night we get something new. I want her injured more often. She went to a luncheon today with wines while I worked. Something ain’t right, yet feels so right. Wine makes everything right! And Scotch.

posts.name: N@th@n V@nd€rgrift

Robert Alfert:

Gracefully this is an infrequent occurrence, but it does seem like when it happens, it’s in lots!

Saturday, a 2009 Huet Franc de Pied was slightly oxidized. Another was quite lovely.

Sunday, a 1995 Kalin Chard was horribly oxidized, looked like a mature Sauternes.

Today, a 2003 Joguet Chinon Clos de le Dioterie, my second one in a few weeks that is horribly stewed, likes spoiled tomatoes. I gagged.

I almost went for the Lagavulin like I did last night, but instead grabbed a Levet, which is like the Lagavulin of Côte Rotie. Sitting by the lake on a lovely breezy evening, dog at my feet, and all is forgotten, after this cathartic!

I feel your pain, I’ve had some real bummers lately: 2005 Fourrier Gruenchers, 1998 Conterno Francia, 2007 Cantalupo Carellae, 2010 Texier St. Julien Pergaud.

posts.name: Todd Hamina
posts.cooked: Paying for those sprints in ways you had yet to imagine.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Todd Hamina:

Paying for those sprints in ways you had yet to imagine.

Lol, and coming up short!!

posts.name: Carlos Delpin
posts.cooked: Hear you. I’m just coming back from a 12 day hospital stay thanks to a burst appendix. Looking forward to trying my luck with a few oldies later this week.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Think of it as inadvertent cellar Spring cleaning…

Last distressingly corked bottles I suffered were a 1990 Pierre Boillot Volnay Santenots and a 1970 Bel Air Marquis d’Aligre, back in February. I am afraid of the karma I am storing up.

posts.name: Charlie Carnes
posts.cooked:
As Arthur said, “I hate when that happens”

I picked my daughter at airport from Clemson today and opened a 2004 Dom Rose that was totally shot. Thank goodness for the NV Camille Saves Rose I had as a backup bottle.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: It’s a joke, Howard. Quote the whole comment for context, i.e., we get free meals!

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked:
Robert Alfert:

It’s a joke, Howard. Quote the whole comment for context, i.e., we get free meals!

Does that mean I cannot give you grief for the comment. Remember, I am (was?) a lawyer also. I can pick out one sentence you say and go right after it.

fancy_title: Cursed by the wines gods, four spoiled wines since Friday
